steps.prompt: Which word is the main finite verb of the question?

hints.veniens: 'Veniens' is a present active participle meaning 'coming'
hints.putas: 'Putas' is a parenthetical verb meaning 'do you think' — the main question verb is 'inveniet'
hints.Filius: 'Filius' is a noun meaning 'Son' — part of the subject

hints.perfect passive participle: A perfect passive participle would end in -tus — 'veniens' ends in -ens
hints.future active participle: A future active participle would be 'venturus' — 'veniens' is present
hints.gerundive: A gerundive would be 'veniendus' — 'veniens' is a present participle

hints.to invent: While English 'invent' derives from this, 'invenire' primarily means 'to find'

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'invenit' — 'inveniet' has the future -ie- formation
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'inveniat' — 'inveniet' is future indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'invenit' (long e) — 'inveniet' is future

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come) — the base verb
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.invito, invitare, invitavi, invitatum: These are the parts of 'invitare' (to invite)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'putes' — 'putas' is indicative

hints.pugno, pugnare, pugnavi, pugnatum: These are the parts of 'pugnare' (to fight)
hints.punio, punire, punivi, punitum: These are the parts of 'punire' (to punish)
hints.posco, poscere, poposci, —: These are the parts of 'poscere' (to demand)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'fides' — 'fidem' with -em is accusative
'Terra' could be nominative, but after 'in' (place where) it is ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'terram' — 'terra' after 'in' is ablative
hints.vocative: 'Terra' is not being addressed — it is ablative after 'in'

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'homo' — 'hominis' with -is is genitive

translation: When the Son of Man comes, do you think he will find faith on earth?
